meta_title: For Founders: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Passive Income to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Stop the cycle of unfinished apps. Learn to build passive income streams using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid, revenue-focused SaaS development.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is 'feature creep' masquerading as perfectionism. By shifting your focus to passive income, you force a lean MVP approach. Use Antigravity to architect the core engine and Claude Code to iterate at high velocity, ensuring you ship a revenue-generating product before losing momentum.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is filled with 'almost finished' projects—monuments to the founder who spent three months on a custom login flow while the actual market problem remained unsolved. You are trapped in the cycle of infinite refinement, losing the war for market share one pixel at a time.

But the pivot to passive income changes the psychology of development. When your code is measured by its ability to generate recurring revenue rather than aesthetic perfection, the bloat disappears. It is time to stop building features and start building a machine that pays for your time.

steps.title: Define the Revenue Engine
steps.description: Identify a singular, high-friction problem you can solve in 14 days. Map the core logic that creates value without unnecessary UI overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Analyze the following problem: [INSERT PROBLEM]. Design a minimal architecture that delivers core value via API, focusing on a subscription-based monetization model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap focusing on the core value proposition and Stripe integration'.

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP with Antigravity
steps.description: Generate the boilerplate and infrastructure to bypass the 'empty folder' syndrome. Focus on database schema and authentication.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean, production-ready SaaS boilerplate using Next.js and Supabase. Include user auth and a basic subscription billing flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure and setup the database schema for user subscriptions'.

steps.title: Automate the Value Delivery
steps.description: Implement the core logic that allows the product to function without active management. This is the 'passive' in your income stream.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated worker function that processes user data requests and triggers a webhook response. Ensure error handling is robust for production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the background worker logic and test the end-to-end data flow'.

steps.title: Integrate Monetization Hooks
steps.description: Hardcode the paywall early. If you aren't collecting, you aren't building a business, you're building a hobby.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function to gate premium features behind a Stripe subscription check. Include a fallback for non-paying users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add Stripe checkout integration and gate premium API endpoints'.

steps.title: Deploy to Production Velocity
steps.description: Use automated deployment pipelines to get your app live. Perfection is the enemy of profit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline for Vercel that includes environment variable management and automated testing for the subscription flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Deploy the current build to production and verify the Stripe test-mode transaction flow'.

pro_tips: Never build custom auth; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 40+ hours of security engineering.
If a feature takes more than 3 days to build, it is likely bloat; kill it or outsource it to an API.
Your first 10 paying customers are worth more than 10,000 lines of code; prioritize the 'Buy' button over the 'Dashboard' UI.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests simultaneously with your features; it prevents technical debt from accumulating during rapid iteration.
Passive income is a myth at launch; build the product to be automated, then spend your time on aggressive distribution, not coding.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, shift your entire focus to distribution. Spend 80% of your time on SEO, cold outreach, and content, and only 20% on feature updates. Your goal is to reach your first MRR milestone before writing another line of code.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in the editor. You have the tools, the architecture, and the strategy to turn your code into an asset that works while you sleep. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
